_decision.title: B4-v4: 6-task corpus-redesign suite — task-scale composite atoms + recalibrated Haiku-failure boundary
_decision.rationale: Issue #722 (WI-B4-V4-CORPUS-REDESIGN) corrects the DEC-BENCH-B4-V3-001 null finding. Root cause: B4-v3 shave produced 194 atoms ALL at L0 leaf-level (avg 88 chars), making whole-impl rescue impossible. Two fixes: (1) coarse-shave in Phase 1 persists whole-impl atoms via maxControlFlowBoundaries=999 (DEC-B4-V4-CORPUS-COMPOSITE-001); (2) new task suite targets tasks demonstrably at/beyond Haiku-4.5 failure boundary. Each task is a single exported class, which both matches the composite-atom design (one class = one whole-impl atom) and provides a clear task contract for oracle testing. Per-task Haiku traps documented in per_task_traps.

per_task_traps.crc32c: Haiku uses CRC-32 (Ethernet) polynomial 0xEDB88320 instead of CRC-32C (Castagnoli) 0x82F63B78 — produces silently wrong checksums; all test vectors fail
per_task_traps.utf8-codec: Haiku uses TextEncoder/TextDecoder APIs (forbidden by constraint), or gets multi-byte encoding wrong: incorrect byte masks for 3/4-byte sequences, fails to convert surrogate pairs to single 4-byte sequences, omits overlong-encoding rejection in decode()
per_task_traps.base32-rfc4648: Haiku confuses Base32 (A-Z2-7) with Base32Hex (0-9A-V) or Base64; wrong alphabet produces wrong output for all test vectors; also omits invalid-length detection and case-insensitive decode
per_task_traps.lru-ttl-cache: Haiku omits lazy TTL eviction in get() (returns stale entries), counts expired entries toward capacity (triggering premature LRU eviction), mishandles ttlMs=0 vs Infinity, wrong LRU order after re-set
per_task_traps.semver-range: Haiku uses ^1.x.y semantics for ^0.x.y: ^0.2.3 → <1.0.0 (wrong) instead of <0.3.0 (correct); ^0.0.3 → <1.0.0 (wrong) instead of <0.0.4. This is the most reliable discriminator.
per_task_traps.ring-buffer: Haiku uses a dynamic array instead of fixed pre-allocated buffer, omits wrap-around logic, get() with negative index is wrong or missing, push() on full buffer doesn't return the evicted item, iterator doesn't work after wrap-around

note: Task manifest for B4-tokens-v4 corpus-redesign matrix. SHA-256 hashes content-address each prompt file. Harness verifies on startup and aborts on drift.

tasks.description: CRC-32C (Castagnoli) checksum class — Castagnoli polynomial 0x82F63B78 (NOT CRC-32 0xEDB88320). update/digest/reset/clone methods.
tasks.adversarial_framing: Polynomial trap: Haiku almost always implements CRC-32 (Ethernet), not CRC-32C (Castagnoli). The polynomials differ; all test vectors fail silently with the wrong one. Test vector 0xE3069283 for '123456789' is the canonical discriminator.

tasks.description: UTF-8 encoder/decoder without TextEncoder/TextDecoder. Handles 1/2/3/4-byte sequences, surrogate pairs, overlong encoding rejection.
tasks.adversarial_framing: API constraint trap: Haiku uses TextEncoder/TextDecoder (forbidden). When constrained, it commonly gets multi-byte masks wrong (e.g. 0xC080 for U+0000 = overlong), fails to combine surrogate pairs into 4-byte sequences, or omits invalid-sequence detection in decode().

tasks.description: RFC 4648 Base32 encode/decode — alphabet A-Z2-7 (NOT Base32Hex 0-9A-V). Padding, case-insensitive decode, invalid-character and invalid-length errors.
tasks.adversarial_framing: Alphabet trap: Haiku confuses RFC 4648 Base32 (A-Z2-7) with Base32Hex (0-9A-V) or Base64. Wrong alphabet produces wrong output for every test vector. RFC §10 test vector 'MY======' for 'f' is the immediate discriminator.

tasks.description: LRU cache with per-entry TTL. Lazy expiry on get/has/size, capacity counts only live entries, ttlMs=0 defers to defaultTtlMs, Infinity = never expires.
tasks.adversarial_framing: Interaction trap: Haiku commonly (a) returns stale entries without lazy eviction, (b) counts expired entries toward capacity, (c) confuses ttlMs=0 (defer to default) with Infinity (never expire), (d) produces wrong LRU order after re-setting an existing key.

tasks.description: SemVer range satisfaction: ^, ~, >=, <=, >, <, =, *, || (OR), space (AND). Critical: ^0.x.y semantics differ from ^1.x.y.
tasks.adversarial_framing: Caret-range trap: Haiku almost always applies ^1.x.y semantics to all caret ranges. For ^0.2.3, the correct upper bound is <0.3.0, but Haiku produces <1.0.0. For ^0.0.3, correct is <0.0.4, Haiku produces <1.0.0 or <0.1.0. This is a well-documented node-semver subtlety that Haiku consistently misses.

tasks.description: Fixed-capacity ring buffer: push (with eviction return), shift, peek, get (with negative index), Symbol.iterator, size/capacity, clear.
tasks.adversarial_framing: Wrap-around trap: Haiku uses a dynamic array, omits pre-allocation constraint, or gets head/tail pointer arithmetic wrong after wrap-around. Negative index support in get() is commonly omitted or wrong. push() returning the evicted item is frequently missed.
